findingDetails.title: Active-goal sanitization is a narrow blacklist before promoting user objective text into system context
findingDetails.claim: A malicious objective can still carry system-prompt-like instructions into the injected active-goal block because the sanitizer only strips a few ASCII role-prefix and instruction-reset patterns before renderGoalInjection promotes the derived goalPrompt into output.system.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked set/replacement, stored normalization, enhancement, injection, and current adversarial tests; all use the same narrow sanitizer and tests cover marker breakout, raw ASCII role labels, two instruction-reset phrase families, and triple backticks only.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The implementation may intentionally treat the active goal as user-authored guidance, but the plugin persists that text and reinjects it as system context, making the sanitizer the protection against durable instruction elevation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the active-goal injection contract is explicitly changed to say user objectives are trusted instructions by design and only marker/format breakout is in scope.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search and direct reads covered sanitizeInlineText, sanitizePromptText, buildEnhancedGoalPrompt, normalizeStoredGoal, setGoal, renderGoalInjection, appendGoalBrief, executeGoalAction, mk-goal-state adversarial tests, and mk-goal-supervisor evidence redaction tests.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: partial: prompt-injection defenses exist but are narrower than the phase 002/007 risk and acceptance language imply
traceabilityChecks.checklist_evidence: notApplicable: scoped phase folders are Level 1 and have no checklist.md
traceabilityChecks.evidence_redaction: pass: verifier evidence is redacted before persistence/status and covered by tests
traceabilityChecks.graph_status: stale: used graphless fallback with exact Grep and direct reads

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: non-trivial plugin security review across sanitizer, persistence, enhancement, injection, status, and tests

targetSelection.selectionReason: Security pass focused on objective sanitization, prompt enhancement ordering, injection rendering, status preview, and evidence redaction surfaces named by the prompt.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Blacklist sanitizer permits broader prompt-control payloads before active goal text is injected into system context.

searchActions.result: Found sanitizer and injection path in mk-goal.js
Adversarial test covers active_goal marker, raw system/developer labels, two instruction reset phrases, and triple backticks only

searchLedger.rationale: The sanitizer is narrower than the prompt-injection mitigation claim for persisted user objectives injected into system context.

searchLedger.invariant: Every reviewed set/replace/read path should sanitize objective text before persistence or injection.

searchActions.result: normalizeStoredGoal and setGoal sanitize objective; executeGoalAction set routes through setGoal

searchLedger.rationale: No separate bypass found; all reviewed objective-entry paths use the same sanitizer.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of set, replacement, stored normalization, and tool set routing.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The deterministic enhancement pipeline could reintroduce unsanitized objective text after setGoal sanitizes it.

searchActions.result: setGoal passes sanitizedObjective into buildEnhancedGoalPrompt, which sanitizes inline objective again and sanitizes the assembled prompt

searchLedger.rationale: Enhancement ordering does not reintroduce raw unsanitized text; it inherits the sanitizer weakness but is not a separate bypass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Sanitization occurs before and during deterministic prompt generation.

searchLedger.invariant: Verifier evidence and status output should not leak common token/API-key secrets.

searchActions.result: Verifier evidence is redacted before storage and status output re-redacts lastEvidence
Tests assert api_key and sk-token samples are absent from stored evidence and status output

searchLedger.rationale: Reviewed evidence/status secret surfaces are covered by implementation and tests.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read confirmed redaction and test coverage for common token/API-key patterns.

title: Active-goal sanitization is a narrow blacklist before promoting user objective text into system context

detail: Phase 002/007 prompt-injection defenses exist but are narrower than the persisted-objective injection risk implies.
Scoped phase folders are Level 1 packets and do not have checklist.md files.

reason: set/replacement/stored-normalization/tool paths all use sanitizeInlineText before persistence or injection.
setGoal passes sanitizedObjective into buildEnhancedGoalPrompt, which sanitizes again and sanitizes the assembled prompt.
verifier evidence is redacted before storage/status and covered by supervisor tests for api_key and sk-token samples.
